canonical_ingress_fixture.summary: The iPad harness decoded MB legacy beacon manufacturer data into normalized legacy_beacon_received lines with beacon_version=1, envelope_version=1, payload_kind=TX, message_id_hash, and sender_peer_id_hash.

full_envelope_capability_probe.summary: No full MX envelope receive was claimed in this run; observed evidence is MB legacy beacon refs only.

hardware_replay_fixture.summary: The hardware log records replay-normalizable fields for MB beacon refs; no full-message delivery, route, ACK, retry, or trust replay fixture is claimed.

ios_background_ble_boundary.summary: All iPad captures were foreground devicectl harness launches; no iOS background scan or advertise behavior is claimed.
ios_background_ble_boundary.test_command: xcrun devicectl device process launch --console dev.meshx.mobile.harness ...

legacy_beacon_gossip_hardware.summary: Fresh iPad auto-beacon run dispatched 28 MB legacy beacon refs from sender ios-harness-B1C7BB9A; Android did not record a matched iPad sender hash, so this is iPad foreground emit evidence, not Android receive proof.
legacy_beacon_gossip_hardware.test_command: xcrun devicectl device process launch --device 39FD8D3A-9CA5-5DEF-AFC0-AA5205511117 --terminate-existing --console dev.meshx.mobile.harness --meshx-auto-beacon

legacy_beacon_observe_hardware.summary: Fresh iPad observe run captured 474 legacy_beacon_received lines, including 247 lines with attached SM-T577U sender hash 12f78557517a13f9; this proves foreground iPad observe of Android MB beacon refs only.
legacy_beacon_observe_hardware.test_command: xcrun devicectl device process launch --device 39FD8D3A-9CA5-5DEF-AFC0-AA5205511117 --terminate-existing --console dev.meshx.mobile.harness --meshx-auto-scan --meshx-log-candidate-discoveries; adb -s R52W90AW7EN shell am start -n dev.meshx.mob/.MainActivity --ez meshx_ble_selftest true

negative_claim_review.summary: Operator review keeps iOS parity, delivery, full-envelope, background, routing, trust, and whole-project completion claims blocked.

target_ios_device_matrix.summary: Coding iPad iPad12,1 on iOS 26.5 was connected over wired CoreDevice; SM-T577U Android 13/API 33 was connected over adb as the Android peer.
target_ios_device_matrix.test_command: xcrun devicectl list devices; adb devices -l

missing_evidence: iOS device matrix for legacy beacon observe, legacy beacon gossip, and full-envelope capability runs.
Observer device metadata for every cross-platform hardware claim.
notes: iOS parity evidence must be device-specific and cannot reuse Android-only proof.
required_evidence: iPhone/iPad model, iOS version, BLE state, app build id, permission state, and foreground/background state for every run.
A matching Android or second MeshX observer entry when validating cross-platform observe or gossip.

missing_evidence: iOS bridge fixture for canonical received_message_beacon.
iOS bridge fixture for canonical received_message when full-envelope participation exists.
notes: Canonical shape is necessary before any iOS hardware log becomes comparable to Android logs.
required_evidence: Native iOS event mapped into canonical received_message_beacon or received_message JSON.
Replay fixture proving the iOS-origin event normalizes through the shared ingress path.

missing_evidence: iOS scan implementation evidence for legacy beacon decode.
iOS hardware capture proving legacy beacon observation.
notes: Observing a beacon remains pointer/ref evidence, not full message delivery.
required_evidence: iOS hardware log showing a compact legacy message beacon observed from Android or another MeshX peer.
Canonical received_message_beacon event with matching message_id_hash, sender_peer_hash, payload_kind, and envelope_version.

missing_evidence: iOS legacy beacon dispatcher or equivalent advert emission implementation.
Observer capture and audit summary for iOS-origin legacy beacon gossip.
notes: Gossip evidence must not claim routing, ACKs, retries, or delivery.
required_evidence: iOS hardware log showing compact legacy beacon emission or gossip.
Second MeshX-capable observer log proving canonical received_message_beacon capture from the iOS device.

missing_evidence: Capability-proven iOS hardware pair or explicit negative capability ledger.
notes: Full-envelope adverts remain capability-proven only; legacy beacons must not be promoted.
required_evidence: iOS BLE capability probe for full-envelope advert payload size and scan compatibility.
Observer log containing canonical received_message if full-envelope advert is supported.

missing_evidence: Artifact metadata with iOS model, version, capture command, and observer role.
notes: Replay normalization remains the canonical ingress proof for hardware captures.
required_evidence: Committed iOS hardware JSONL fixture or validation ledger with raw capture reference.
Replay test proving fixture normalizes through the same canonical replay path.

missing_evidence: Background capability and hardware logs if iOS background BLE is required.
notes: iOS advert-only foreground parity and iOS background BLE are separate claims.
required_evidence: Explicit foreground-only or background-capable policy for iOS advert-only participation.
Core Bluetooth background capability and hardware evidence if background participation is claimed.

missing_evidence: Operator review preventing Android evidence from satisfying iOS claims.
notes: Negative fixtures must replace pure claim gates before any iOS parity release claim.
required_evidence: Implementation-backed negative fixtures for bridge shell only, Android evidence reuse, missing dispatcher, unproven capability, and missing replay fixture.
Release-note review preserving blocked iOS parity wording until every relevant gate passes.

notes: iOS remains contract-only until native advert-only behavior and hardware evidence exist.
Android hardware evidence cannot satisfy iOS parity gates.
This plan adds evidence gates only; it does not change iOS runtime behavior.
Ready evidence means the supplied metadata covers iOS advert-only hardware validation gates.
This review does not enable iOS scanner, advertiser, background BLE, full-envelope advert, gossip, or parity claims.
Android hardware evidence cannot satisfy iOS parity; iOS remains contract-only until implementation-backed device evidence passes.
